title: Introduction & Synergetic Settings Overview
summary: Host introduces the OmniPro 220 multi-process welder and explains synergetic (auto-set) settings: the operator selects a welding process and material thickness, and the machine automatically configures the remaining parameters. Host states the plan to time each process setup and perform a test weld for each.
transcript: get welding in a matter of minutes I've got the Omni Pro 220 it's got synergetic settings more and more welders are coming out with this you know I know the fillers had it forever with their Auto set welders but pretty much what this means is all you have to do is choose the process and then the thickness of material you want to do and the Machine does everything for you after that so let's just see how easy it really is I'm going to time myself setting up the machine for each process and then obviously do a little test just to see how good those settings are

title: Flux Core Setup & Weld Test
summary: Starting from a new spool not installed and all leads disconnected, flux core setup is completed in 4.5 minutes. A flat horizontal weld on 3/16-inch plate is tested first, then a fillet weld on 1/8-inch (approx. 11-gauge) coupons. Wire used is .035 Vulcan flux core. The OmniPro settings range from gauge 12 to 10 for that thickness; travel speed is adjusted accordingly. Both welds are described as turning out fine.
transcript: starting with flux core with a new spool not installed and all the leads disconnected it took me four and a half minutes from start to finish since that first test of flat horizontal weld on 3 16 inch plate turned out nice I kicked it down a notch and did a fillet weld on some welding coupons I am using .035 Vulcan flux core wire now if you want to get really nitpicky these are eighth inch coupons and technically an eighth inch is about 11 gauge and so the omnipro settings go from 12 to 10. so you know go a little slower if you choose 12 and quicker if 10. both those welds turned out just fine with the flux cord

title: MIG Setup & Weld Test
summary: MIG process setup takes approximately 5.5 minutes. Key steps noted include swapping the drive roller from a knurled wheel to a V-groove (both included), running the wire the same as flux core, connecting 100% CO2 shielding gas, and switching the polarity leads (incorrect leads produce poor welds). Wire used is .035 solid core Vulcan wire. The OmniPro display shows MIG Steel C100 with diameter and thickness selection. Synergetic settings are described as right on for the MIG welds.
transcript: for the Mig process it took just a minute longer I was right at about five and a half minutes there are a couple little extras you know don't forget to switch out the roller from a knurled wheel to the v-groove the Omni Pro does come with both running the wire is just the same as Flex score but don't forget the gas I'm using a hundred percent CO2 more importantly well okay the other stuff's important too but don't forget to switch those leads yes you will get some pretty ugly welds and they won't turn out great if not the wire I'm using is .035 solid core Vulcan wire foreign I would say that the suggested settings or the synergetic settings were right on for the Mig welds as well

title: Stick Setup & Weld Test
summary: Stick is described as the easiest setup because there is no wire to run; connecting the ground clamp and electrode holder takes approximately 30 seconds. The machine prompts for 60- or 70-series rod selection. Host notes uncertainty about 6010 rod compatibility. Welds are performed using 7018 1/8-inch rod on 3/16-inch flat plate and then on 1/8-inch coupons in a T-joint fillet configuration.
transcript: stick is by far the easiest setup you're not dealing with wire and so hooking up the ground and Electro holder takes a whopping 30 seconds it does ask if you're using 60 or 70 series rods but no ending so I'm not sure if it's capable of running a 6010 Rod but with this technology and the Machine I'm assuming it can tell me if I'm wrong I'm using some 7018 eighth inch rod and this first example was done on some 3 16 inch flat plate and then I moved it over to the eighth inch coupons doing a fillet weld on a t-joint foreign

title: TIG Setup & Weld Test
summary: TIG setup takes the longest at approximately 8 minutes due to torch assembly, tungsten grinding, and gas hookup. The machine uses lift-start TIG (not high-frequency start): the tungsten is touched to the workpiece, the foot pedal is pressed, and the arc initiates upon lifting the tungsten. The foot pedal provides amperage control throughout the weld. Scratch-start operation without the foot pedal is also possible. Host evaluates the preset synergetic settings as good on 1/8-inch coupons and positions the machine in the middle of the TIG feature spectrum.
transcript: you can run the machine as a scratch start without the foot pedal but I'd opted for it because it does give you the amperage control once you add in the time for the torch setup tungsten grinding and gas hookup it does take the longest I was right at about eight minutes Harbor Freight if you're listening well I know you're not but hey one thing that would really take the omnipro up to the next level is adding high frequency start you do get lift style TIG so to start the arc you touch the tungsten to the work piece step on the foot pedal and as soon as you lift up thus the name the arc is created and you can start welding and as mentioned before you can control the amperage with the foot pedal foreign these are on just some eighth inch coupons and I would say the machine actually has some pretty good preset settings so I would consider the omnipro right in the middle of the spectrum as far as the tig features go

title: Conclusion & Overall Assessment
summary: Host summarizes that the synergetic settings allow quality welds to be laid down within minutes across all four processes, and concludes that the OmniPro 220 is worth keeping.
transcript: all right I'll say it throwing down some awesome welds within a couple minutes is pretty freaking sweet the omniprobe I would say is probably worth keeping around that's all I got for this one thanks for watching we'll see you next time
